title: chrysanthemum lavandulifolium var. tomentellum
intro: Chrysanthemum lavandulifolium var. tomentellum, also known as the woolly-leaved chrysanthemum, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the mountainous regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum lavandulifolium var. tomentellum include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve circulation, and reduce symptoms of fever and respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
